mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 840d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 840d (B57) engine typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,000 depending on location and workshop rates. A blown engine on a premium BMW can cost £9,000–£13,000 to replace. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

Yes — the B57 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable diesel engines, 'celebrated for its robust performance, impressive efficiency, and excellent reliability'. It's a significant improvement over the N57 that preceded it. However, common issues include timing chain tensioner wear, EGR cooler coolant leaks (subject to BMW recalls), and DPF clogging from short urban journeys.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 840d?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Timing chain tensioner wear — causing cold-start rattle, power loss and increased fuel consumption, (2) EGR cooler coolant leaks — subject to global BMW recalls, and (3) DPF clogging — particularly on vehicles used for short urban journeys.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 840d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £30,000–£50,000 840d, a £4,500–£6,500 recon is 10–20% of the car's value — worth it given the 8 Series's strong retained values. For higher-mileage examples, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 840d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 840d uses the B57D30 engine family. Early cars (2018–2020) have B57D30T0 or B57D30B (320 bhp, 680 Nm); LCI cars (2020–present) have B57D30T2 (340 bhp, 700 Nm with 48V MHEV). The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Does the B57 have timing chain issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: While the B57 is significantly more reliable than the N47 and early N57 engines, timing chain tensioner wear is still documented. Symptoms include metallic rattling noises on cold start, rough idling and engine misfires. BMW recommends regular oil changes using the correct BMW Longlife-04 spec oil to minimise wear. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

3.0L twin-turbo with 48V MHEV — 340 bhp, 700 Nm. Mild-hybrid system adds efficiency

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an 840d engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The twin-turbo layout and complex emissions systems add labour time.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (B57 Engine Family)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or metallic noise on cold start, engine warning light, loss of power, increased fuel consumption
problems.typicalMileage: 50,000–80,000 miles — can occur earlier on higher-mileage examples

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: While the B57 is a significant improvement over the N47 and early N57 engines, timing chain tensioner wear is still a documented issue. Symptoms include metallic rattling noises on cold start, rough idling and engine misfires. One 840d owner reported "problems with the timing chain, leading to an engine warning light, loss of power, increased fuel consumption and a metallic noise on cold start". At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately. A £1,200–£2,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£9,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/b57-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: EGR Cooler Coolant Leak
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning, coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, rough idling, burning smell from engine bay
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — a global recall affects BMW diesel engines including B57-equipped models
problems.repairCost: £0 (free under recall) / £600–£1,200 (out of warranty — EGR cooler replacement)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: verify recall completion status immediately; coolant loss can cause serious engine damage

problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler is a well-known vulnerability across BMW's diesel engine range, including the B57. A 2020 840d owner reported "I've been observing the DPF filter via OBD... the DPF shows soot at 82%, quickly reached 100%". BMW has issued multiple recalls affecting diesel vehicles globally over EGR cooler faults that could cause coolant leaks. Verify recall completion before buying any 840d — this is a free fix at the dealership.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

problems.title: DPF Clogging & Regeneration Issues
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced performance, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light, limp mode
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — short urban journeys accelerate the issue
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: A 2020 840d owner with 120,000 km reported DPF soot levels reaching 100%, indicating severe clogging. Like all modern diesels, the 840d's DPF is sensitive to short urban journeys that prevent the engine from reaching optimal temperatures for regeneration. If you primarily drive short distances, factor in regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regenerations. Some owners report power returning to normal after a forced regeneration. Ignoring DPF warnings can lead to clogged filters, requiring expensive replacement.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 840d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with BMW-approved oil changes (BMW Longlife-04 spec)
* Confirmed EGR recall completion — this affects BMW diesel engines globally
* Evidence of regular motorway use (DPF health)
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — these are complex performance diesels
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle — this is a warning sign of timing chain tensioner wear
* No proof of EGR recall work — this is a free fix; lack of it suggests neglect
* Poor service history — the B57 is reliable but needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 840d's B57 engine is one of BMW's finest modern diesels — a 3.0-litre twin-turbo inline-six with up to 340 bhp and 700 Nm that delivers effortless performance and genuine refinement. It's "celebrated for its robust performance, impressive efficiency, and excellent reliability". The B57 is a significant improvement over the N57 that preceded it, with better timing chain durability. However, timing chain tensioner wear, EGR cooler recalls, and DPF sensitivity are well-documented issues. Buy with full service history, confirmed recall work, and evidence of regular long-distance driving — and you'll have one of the most rewarding grand tourers on the road.

cta.label: → Compare 840d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 840d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic noise (timing chain tensioner warning)
* EGR Recall — Verify EGR cooler recall completion — this is critical and affects BMW diesel engines globally
* DPF Health — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys are a red flag
* Service History — Including the B57 engine and confirmed BMW-approved oil changes
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the engine; B57s are generally tight but check anyway
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for timing, EGR or DPF-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
